name: Wave Table
explanation: A polyphonic wavetable synthesizer in the spirit of early-1980s German digital hardware. Eight single-cycle waves are generated in code, spanning a pure sine through ever richer harmonic spectra into bright, hollow formant shapes. A Wave Position control scans smoothly through the bank with linear interpolation between tables, morphing the timbre in real time. Each of up to eight voices runs a wavetable oscillator into a resonant 4-pole low-pass shaped by its own filter envelope and amount, then an amplitude envelope, so chords ring with independent contours. Controls: Wave Position, Cutoff, Resonance, Filter Env Amount, Attack, Release, Detune and Level.
